body_txt: Our old friends Alice and Bob are in a specific type of close relationship. Specifically, she provides him with the emotional , psychological , and/or moral support to function when times are tough (sometimes just to function at all ). She might be his first good friend , a Love Interest , a psychiatrist/priest Confidant , or a full-blown Caretaker to Bob, serving as a Morality Chain or Kid with the Leash to rein in Bob's darker impulses, deliver pep talks and administer the occasional Cooldown Hug . Summed up, whenever Alice is around, Bob is a better, happier person. Naturally , she will usually get taken out of the story to break up the equilibrium and get the plot moving. Kidnapping is traditional , but murder is not out of the question. Bob's reaction will be one of two extremes: psychotic Unstoppable Rage on par with The Hulk in the throes of withdrawal from Gamma-Cocaine , or comatose gibbering ; see also Went Crazy When They Left . The middle road is Tranquil Fury as Bob plots his deadly, determined vengeance. While most stories emphasize that Alice does this because of how deeply she cares for Bob, there are darker takes on this trope. If Bob is psychotically possessive , he will go to any lengths to protect Alice from perceived threats, culminating in destroying everyone she loves so she must remain in their All Take and No Give 'relationship.' This is especially tragic if Alice genuinely wants to help him, but he's already sliding too far to help. When she inevitably leaves him/is rescued, Bob's mental state takes a nosedive. Sometimes, Alice will be able to introduce Bob to other friends, lessening his dependence on her. Very rarely, she will be able to cure him of his dependence entirely , or give him the necessary tools and resolve to survive when she's gone. This trope has some basis in Real Life . People like Bob might be diagnosed with dependent personality disorder or, if they're cripplingly shy around most others , avoidant personality disorder. Compare The Heart , when the character is this to the others in their group.
